channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: House committee releases new Epstein documents, including ex-Trump secretary interview
item.description: (The Hill) -- The House Oversight Committee on Friday published fresh files connected to the late Jeffrey Epstein’s criminal dealings, including the transcript of a previous interview with former Labor Secretary Alex Acosta, who served under President Trump during his first term.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: I-5 freeway to temporarily shut down for live fire exercise at Camp Pendleton
item.description: Governor Gavin Newsom has criticized the federal government for allowing a live-fire military demonstration at Camp Pendleton to take place near the heavily trafficked I-5 freeway, prompting a temporary closure of the highway and causing potential disruption to the local economy.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky says Trump didn’t say ‘yes’ to Tomahawk missiles request
item.description: Zelensky did not provide an exact reason as to why Ukraine’s request for Tomahawk missiles was denied.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Burning ship loaded with gas abandoned in Gulf of Aden off Yemen as 1 mariner missing
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A Cameroonian-flagged ship caught fire Saturday in the Gulf of Aden off Yemen after possibly being struck by a projectile, officials said, with at least one mariner missing and another still likely aboard the burning tanker after the rest of the crew abandoned the
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: OSBI investigation continues into 2023 cold case of Heather Patrick
item.description: The Oklahoma State Bureau of Investigations (OSBI) is seeking information regarding the suspicious disappearance of Heather Patrick.
item.dc:creator: Terré Gables/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: Legally blind Ohio artist says she can't drive, but she can tattoo
item.description: Nicole Morrone was born legally blind, but she still creates detailed, colorful artwork -- on her clients' skin.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Cloutier, KREX 5

item.title: Live updates as 'No Kings’ protests underway
item.description: The rallies are taking place during the government shutdown as well federal troop deployments across the country. Follow this story for live updates.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, NewsNation

item.title: Shutdown impact: What it means for workers, federal programs and the economy
item.description: The federal government shutdown is approaching the second longest on record and there's no end in sight.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 5,500 truckers fail English skills test, lose driving privileges in US, industry leader says
item.description: "So far, we've registered 5,500 out of service and suspended licenses, we are seeing harder and more stringent enforcement," said Delgado Vallejo.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After Charlie Kirk’s assassination, left-wing professors double down on shredding his legacy
item.description: College faculty members attack Charlie Kirk's legacy after his assassination, making unsubstantiated claims about violence stemming from his conservative activism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alaska storm damage so bad many evacuees won’t go home for at least 18 months, governor says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Damage to remote Alaska villages hammered by flooding last weekend is so extreme that many of the more than 2,000 people displaced won’t be able to return to their homes for at least 18 months, Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mother of missing girl previously not seen for 1 year being 'uncooperative,' sheriff says
item.description: The Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office is continuing its search for 9-year-old Melodee Buzzard, who was not previously seen for more than a year and hadn't been photographed in at least two.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Texas teens arrested in killing of Marine veteran working as rideshare driver
item.description: Harris County authorities arrested three teenage boys in the killing of Marine veteran Quoc "Jake" Nguyen, who was shot while driving for Uber.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: With no takers yet, White House meets with colleges still weighing an agreement with Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The five universities that were still weighing President Donald Trump’s higher-education compact were asked to join a White House call Friday to discuss the proposed deal.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Five cities hit with federal presence remain on edge
item.description: (NewsNation) — President Donald Trump has touted the success of his deployment of the National Guard and other federal agents to crack down on immigration and crime, but residents in these cities say this presence has damaged their sense of safety.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump-Zelensky meeting
item.description: President Trump hosted Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky on Friday at the White House, part of a renewed push to end Russia’s over three-years long war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, ABC 4

item.title: Trio wearing Halloween costumes caught on camera terrorizing family while attempting break-in
item.description: A trio wearing Halloween masks was caught on camera terrorizing a family while attempting to break into their home.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Kansas City street being named after America's first Black female millionaire
item.description: "Sarah Rector's story is one of resilience, prosperity and breaking barriers," said Councilwoman Melissa Patterson Hazley, who sponsored the ordinance.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, NewsNation

item.title: Indiana University fires student newspaper adviser who refused to block news stories
item.description: Tension between Indiana University and its student newspaper flared this week with the elimination of the outlet's print editions and the firing of a faculty adviser, who refused an order to keep news stories out of a homecoming edition.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Arizona executes a man for killing 4 members of a family in Phoenix in 1993
item.description: FLORENCE, Arizona (AP) — A man convicted of killing four members of a Phoenix family over 30 years ago in an act of revenge over stolen goods was put to death Friday in Arizona’s second execution of the year. Richard Kenneth Djerf, 55, died by lethal injection for the killings of Albert Luna Sr.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD and SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: From bills to berets: Notable moments from NYC's contentious mayoral debate
item.description: New York City’s mayoral candidates have duked it out in their first general election debate, with contenders Zohran Mamdani, Andrew Cuomo and Curtis Sliwa sparring over everything from the Israel-Hamas war to the city’s education system.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: LA county reaches tentative $828M agreement to settle 400 more claims of sexual abuse
item.description: Los Angeles County has reached a tentative $828 million agreement to settle more than 400 additional sexual abuse claims against county employees that were not included in an earlier settlement that was the largest sexual abuse settlement in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How Trump's approval shifted after the ceasefire in Gaza, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More U.S. adults support President Donald Trump's handling of the conflict in Israel after he brokered a ceasefire deal in Gaza, a new AP-NORC poll finds — but his approval ratings on domestic issues remain weak.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prince Andrew to give up royal title amid Epstein controversy
item.description: Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: South Carolina schedules execution for man who taunted police with message in victim's blood
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina inmate who killed a man, burned his eyes with cigarettes and then painted “catch me if u can” on the wall with the victim's blood more than 20 years ago has been scheduled to be executed next month.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Video shows masked trio in Halloween costumes terrorize family in attempted home invasion
item.description: Video captures three suspects in Halloween masks attempting to break into an Alexandria, Virginia, woman's home while allegedly threatening the family inside.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UFO? Military operation? Questions surround balloon seen above Colorado mountains
item.description: Colorado is no stranger to strange objects floating or flying across the sky (remember "Balloon Boy" from October 2009?), and many residents wanted answers for a strange balloon seen floating through the state on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, Fox 5

item.title: Meta adding AI chatbot safety features for teens
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Instagram and Facebook, plans to roll out new safety features for its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Disapproval of Trump rises in new Emerson College Polling survey: Political Power Ranker
item.description: President Donald Trump's disapproval rating continued to rise this month in the latest Emerson College Polling survey.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: Evacuees detail harrowing scenes of flooding in coastal Alaska villages as airlift continues
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The house rocked as if an earthquake had struck, and suddenly it was floating. Water seeped in through the front door, and waves smacked the big glass window.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Many swings, few knockout hits as Cuomo and Mamdani trade jabs in heated first NYC mayoral debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo entered Thursday evening’s first New York mayoral debate trying to blunt Democratic front-runner Zohran Mamdani's momentum.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Video: Chicago native says armed agents abducted him and questioned citizenship
item.description: A U.S. citizen says armed Border Patrol agents snatched him off a Chicago street in broad daylight early Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Brónagh Tumulty, KRON4

item.title: Gun safety advocates warn of a surge in untraceable 3D-printed weapons in the US
item.description: As police departments around the country report a surge in 3D-printed firearms turning up at crime scenes, gun safety advocates and law enforcement officials are warning that a new generation of untraceable weapons could soon eclipse the “ghost guns” that have already flooded U.S. streets.
item.dc:creator: CLAUDIA LAUER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The world is on track to add 57 superhot days a year, but it could have been worse: Study
item.description: The world is on track to add nearly two months of dangerous superhot days each year by the end of the century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Aldi starts Thanksgiving meal price war: $40 for 10 people
item.description: Aldi is selling “21 total products and ingredients” that the buyer prepares for this year’s Turkey Day.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, KRON4

item.title: Family members share Diane Keaton's cause of death
item.description: Family members of legendary actress Diane Keaton, who passed away Saturday at the age of 79, revealed the Oscar-winning actress died during a battle with pneumonia, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Journalists turn in access badges, exit Pentagon rather than agree to new reporting rules
item.description: Dozens of reporters turned in access badges and exited the Pentagon on Wednesday rather than agree to government-imposed restrictions on their work, pushing journalists who cover the American military further from the seat of its power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
